249	
250	\paragraph{The gap and route~(e), superseded.} The substrate
251	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\) of this chapter is the
252	sheafification of \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\), and
253	sheafification of presheaves of modules is exactly the localization
254	\(L = \mathtt{sheafification}\) at the locally-bijective class \(W = J.W\). Route~(e)
255	proposed to obtain a \emph{full} monoidal structure on
256	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\)
257	by applying \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) (ii) to the already-monoidal category (i),
258	which would require the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (its two
259	whiskering-stability fields \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}, with load-bearing
260	local-injectivity half \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}). This is
261	\emph{not} how the group law is realized. The group law consumes only the
262	\emph{existence} of the three coherence isomorphisms, and the associator is built
263	directly by gluing canonical local isomorphisms through
264	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}); it never invokes
265	the \emph{full} \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance or a coherent monoidal category.
266	The \emph{full monoidal-category} route~(e) is therefore off the critical path. The
267	associator does, however, consume a \emph{single} left-whisker existence lemma
268	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk}, closed by the d.2 stalk--tensor
269	commutation; so the stalk apparatus (d.1/d.2) itself is on the critical path, even
270	though the full coherent monoidal category built from it is not.
271	
272	The stalk-infrastructure analysis that route~(e) turned on is recorded here for
273	reference only, since that route is superseded. The \emph{stalk module structure}
274	is already present in Mathlib: the file
275	\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk} supplies, for a topological
276	space \(X\), a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) on \(X\) and a point
277	\(x \in X\), the \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-module structure on the stalk
278	\(\mathtt{stalk}\,M.\mathtt{presheaf}\,x\) of a presheaf of modules \(M\),
279	together with the germ--scalar compatibility \(\mathtt{germ\_smul}\); a linearity
280	packaging of the induced stalk map of a morphism was built project-side
281	(\cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}, the \emph{linearity half} of ingredient (d.1)).
282	Route~(e) additionally required a (d.1)-\emph{bridge} between the site-level
283	\(J.W\) and the topological stalkwise-isomorphism criterion on
284	\(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\), and (d.2) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
285	\otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk with the relative module-presheaf tensor; there is
286	moreover no monoidal \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) in Mathlib. The full \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) coherent monoidal category that route~(e) would
287	build is not pursued; but the (d.1)-linearity packaging and the (d.2) stalk--tensor
288	commutation are \emph{on} the critical path, since the associator's single open
289	left-whisker obligation \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk} is closed
290	precisely by (d.2) (\cref{sec:tensorobj_stalk_tensor}). What is superseded is only the
291	\emph{pair}-of-whiskering-fields \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) packaging and the
292	\(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal category, not the stalk infrastructure.
293	
294	The project's policy on this gap is \emph{project-side}: the substrate is
295	built inside \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/} (the file
296	\(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean}\) is its home)
297	and the consumer instance is closed against it. A parallel Mathlib upstream
298	PR with the same content is welcome but not on the critical path for the
299	project; the consumer is closed against the project-side substrate
300	independently of any upstream timing.

754	    ⟨tensorObj_middleFour M M' N N' ≪≫ tensorObjIsoOfIso e e' ≪≫ tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
755	
756	/-- **Step 4 — the structure sheaf is `⊗`-invertible** (blueprint
757	`lem:isinvertible_unit`). Witness `𝒪_X`, iso `tensorObj_unit_iso`. -/
758	theorem isInvertible_unit {X : Scheme.{u}} :
759	    IsInvertible (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :=
760	  ⟨SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, ⟨tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
761	
762	/-- **Step 5 — the tensor inverse is determined up to isomorphism** (blueprint
763	`lem:isinvertible_inverse_welldef`). If `M ⊗ N ≅ 𝒪_X` and `M ⊗ N' ≅ 𝒪_X` then
764	`N ≅ N'`, via the inverse-of-inverse chain. -/
765	theorem IsInvertible.inverse_unique {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N N' : X.Modules}
766	    (e : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
767	    (e' : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N' ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
768	    Nonempty (N ≅ N') :=
769	  ⟨(tensorObj_right_unitor N).symm ≪≫
770	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl N) e'.symm ≪≫
771	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
772	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding N M ≪≫ e) (Iso.refl N') ≪≫
773	    tensorObj_left_unitor N'⟩
774	
775	/-- The setoid of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules: `M ∼ M'` iff there exists an
776	isomorphism `M ≅ M'` (blueprint `def:pic_carrier`). -/
777	instance picSetoid (X : Scheme.{u}) : Setoid {M : X.Modules // IsInvertible M} where
778	  r M M' := Nonempty ((M : X.Modules) ≅ (M' : X.Modules))
779	  iseqv :=
780	    ⟨fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ ⟨f⟩ => ⟨e ≪≫ f⟩⟩
781	
782	/-- **Step 2 — the invertibility-carrier Picard group** (blueprint
783	`def:pic_carrier`): the quotient of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules by isomorphism. -/
784	def PicGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ := Quotient (picSetoid X)
785	
786	/-- Multiplication on `PicGroup X`: `[M] · [M'] := [M ⊗_X M']`, well-defined on
787	iso-classes by bifunctoriality (`tensorObjIsoOfIso`), landing in `PicGroup` by
788	`IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
789	noncomputable def picMul {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X → PicGroup X → PicGroup X :=
790	  Quotient.lift₂
791	    (fun a b => Quotient.mk _ ⟨tensorObj a.1 b.1, a.2.tensorObj b.2⟩)
792	    (by
793	      rintro ⟨a, ha⟩ ⟨b, hb⟩ ⟨a', ha'⟩ ⟨b', hb'⟩ ⟨ea⟩ ⟨eb⟩
794	      exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObjIsoOfIso ea eb⟩)
795	
796	/-- The inverse class on `PicGroup X`: `[M] ↦ [N]` for the membership witness `N`
797	of `IsInvertible M`, well-defined by `IsInvertible.inverse_unique`. -/
798	noncomputable def picInv {X : Scheme.{u}} : PicGroup X → PicGroup X :=
799	  Quotient.lift
800	    (fun a => Quotient.mk _
801	      ⟨Classical.choose a.2,
802	        a.1, ⟨tensorObj_braiding _ a.1 ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec a.2).some⟩⟩)
803	    (by
804	      rintro ⟨a, ha⟩ ⟨a', ha'⟩ ⟨ea⟩
805	      refine Quotient.sound ?_
806	      -- both `Classical.choose ha` and `Classical.choose ha'` are tensor inverses of `a`
807	      have h1 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha) ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
808	        (Classical.choose_spec ha).some
809	      have h2 : tensorObj a (Classical.choose ha') ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
810	        tensorObjIsoOfIso ea (Iso.refl _) ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec ha').some
811	      exact IsInvertible.inverse_unique h1 h2)
812	
813	/-- **Step 6 — the invertibility-carrier Picard group is abelian** (blueprint
814	`thm:pic_commgroup`). `[M] · [M'] := [M ⊗_X M']`, `1 := [𝒪_X]`, and `[M]⁻¹` the
815	class of any membership witness of `IsInvertible M`. Each group axiom is a single
816	existence-of-isomorphism: unit laws ← unitors, associativity ← associator,
817	commutativity ← braiding, left inverse ← the witness iso. No monoidal coherence. -/
818	noncomputable instance picCommGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : CommGroup (PicGroup X) where
819	  mul := picMul
820	  one := Quotient.mk _ ⟨SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, isInvertible_unit⟩
821	  inv := picInv
822	  mul_assoc := by
823	    rintro a b c
824	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
825	    induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
826	    induction c using Quotient.ind with | _ c => ?_
827	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_assoc_iso⟩
828	  one_mul := by
829	    rintro a
830	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
831	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_left_unitor a.1⟩
832	  mul_one := by
833	    rintro a
834	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
835	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_right_unitor a.1⟩
836	  inv_mul_cancel := by
837	    rintro a
838	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
839	    exact Quotient.sound
840	      ⟨tensorObj_braiding (Classical.choose a.2) a.1 ≪≫ (Classical.choose_spec a.2).some⟩
841	  mul_comm := by
842	    rintro a b
843	    induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
844	    induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
845	    exact Quotient.sound ⟨tensorObj_braiding a.1 b.1⟩
846	
847	/-! ## §6. Pullback-monoidality substrate (A.1.c): `IsInvertible.pullback`
848	
849	Project-local Mathlib supplement. The relative Picard consumer re-bases onto the
850	`IsInvertible` carrier and its structure maps are *pullback* maps for GENERAL scheme
851	morphisms (the projection `C ×_S T → T` and base-change maps are neither open
852	immersions nor flat). We need that pullback preserves `⊗`-invertibility. This requires
853	`pullbackTensorIso` (`f^*(M ⊗ N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) and `pullbackUnitIso`
854	(`f^*𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_Y`). Blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`. -/
855	
856	/-- **Composition coherence of the unit→pushforward-unit comparison.**
857	
858	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
859	`unitToPushforwardObjUnit` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons
860	of `f` and `h` and the pushforward pseudofunctor coherence `pushforwardComp`. This is
861	the *pushforward-side* (right-adjoint) half of the composition coherence; it is
862	concrete (sectionwise it is just functoriality of the structure-sheaf ring maps,
863	hence `rfl` after the `ext`-chain) and is the pushforward-side input from which the
864	genuinely-needed *pullback-side* coherence of `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is obtained by
865	adjunction-mate transport. Mathlib-absent at the pinned commit. -/
866	lemma unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
867	    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
868	      SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom ≫
869	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).map
870	          (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
871	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app (SheafOfModules.unit Z.ringCatSheaf) := by
872	  apply SheafOfModules.Hom.ext
873	  apply PresheafOfModules.hom_ext
874	  intro U
875	  apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
876	  apply LinearMap.ext
877	  intro a
878	  rfl
879	
880	/-- **Composition coherence of the unit comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`
881	(the genuinely-new ingredient for `pullbackUnitIso`).**
882	
883	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X`, the canonical comparison
884	`f^*𝒪 ⟶ 𝒪` of the composite `h ≫ f` factors through the comparisons of `f` and `h`
885	and the pullback pseudofunctor coherence `pullbackComp`:
886	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h≫f) = (pullbackComp h f).inv ≫ (pullback h).map (pbu f) ≫ pbu h`.
887	
888	This is the pullback-side (left-adjoint) composition coherence — Mathlib-absent at the
889	pinned commit and NOT a sectionwise statement (the abstract left-adjoint pullback has no
890	sectionwise value). It is obtained by adjunction-mate transport from the pushforward-side
891	coherence `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp`: transposing both sides under
892	`pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h≫f)`, the left side becomes `unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h≫f)`
893	and the right side is reassembled via the conjugate/mate identity
894	`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` (relating `pullbackComp.inv` to `pushforwardComp.hom`),
895	`unit_conjugateEquiv`, and the composite-adjunction unit `Adjunction.comp_unit_app`.
896	
897	Consumed by `pullbackUnitIso`: on an affine chart `V` the inclusion `V.ι ≫ f` factors as
898	`g ≫ U.ι` with `Opens.map g.base` `Final`, so two applications of this coherence (one for
899	each factorisation) express the restricted global comparison as a composite of isomorphisms
900	(`pbu` for an open immersion / a `Final`-chart morphism is an iso), whence
901	`pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an iso by `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`.
902	
903	The proof uses `erw` for the associativity / functoriality steps because the `SheafOfModules`
904	category compositions appear in defeq-but-not-syntactic forms (`Scheme.Modules.pullback f`
905	vs `SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom`) on which plain `rw [Category.assoc]` /
906	`rw [Functor.map_comp]` fail to unify. -/
907	lemma pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (h : Z ⟶ Y) (f : Y ⟶ X) :
908	    SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (h ≫ f).toRingCatSheafHom =
909	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≫
910	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback h).map (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f.toRingCatSheafHom) ≫
911	      SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit h.toRingCatSheafHom := by
912	  have key := unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp h f
913	  have conj := unit_conjugateEquiv
914	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
915	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h))
916	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f))
917	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
918	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv] at conj
919	  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _ |>.injective
920	  have hL : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h ≫ f)).homEquiv _ _

1001	functor `Opens.map f.base` is `Final`, and that functor is **always** `Final`: the

1003	representably flat, whence `final_of_representablyFlat` supplies `(Opens.map f.base).Final`

1005	chart-chase contemplated by the blueprint proof — and the iter-240 coherence linchpin

1099	/-- **The presheaf-of-modules pushforward is lax monoidal** (the analogist-named `μ_G`,
1100	Mathlib-absent at the pin). For a morphism `φ : S₀ ⋙ forget₂ ⟶ F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`
1101	of presheaves of *commutative* rings, `PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ` unfolds to
1102	`pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, the composite of the strong-monoidal
1103	topological pushforward `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` (Mathlib) and the lax-monoidal
1104	`restrictScalars φ` (project `restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`), hence lax monoidal by
1105	`Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp`.
1106	
1107	The hypothesis necessarily uses the *inner* `forget₂` association (`F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂)`,
1108	the form `PresheafOfModules.pushforward` expects), but the monoidal-category instance on the
1109	middle presheaf is keyed on the *outer* association `(F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂` (the form
1110	`pushforward₀OfCommRingCat`'s target carries). The two are defeq; bridging them with a local
1111	`MonoidalCategory` instance triggers a kernel-rejected diamond, so instead `φ` is defeq-cast
1112	to the outer form (`φ'`) for the `restrictScalars` factor, and the resulting composite — defeq
1113	to `pushforward φ` — is transported back by `exact`. This is the right-adjoint lax structure
1114	that an eventual oplax comparison `δ` on the abstract left-adjoint pullback would inherit.
1115	Project-local supplement; reusable for the general pullback-monoidality build. -/
1116	noncomputable instance presheafPushforwardLaxMonoidal
1117	    {C D : Type u} [Category.{u} C] [Category.{u} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
1118	    {R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {S₀ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
1119	    (φ : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1120	      F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) :
1121	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal := by
1122	  let φ' : (S₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) ⟶
1123	      (F.op ⋙ R₀) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat := φ
1124	  have h : (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R₀ ⋙
1125	      PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ').LaxMonoidal := inferInstance
1126	  exact h

1142	      F.op ⋙ (R₀ ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))

302	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
303	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
304	
305	\begin{definition}
306	
307	\leanok
308	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
309	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
310	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
311	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
312	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
313	  \[
314	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
315	  \]
316	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
317	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
318	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
319	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
320	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
321	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
322	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
323	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
324	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
325	  canonical compatibility
326	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
327	     \;\cong\;
328	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
329	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
330	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
331	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
332	
333	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
334	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
335	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
336	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
337	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
338	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
339	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
340	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
341	\end{definition}
342	
343	\begin{lemma}
344	
345	\leanok
346	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
347	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
348	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
349	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
350	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
351	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
352	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
353	  \[
354	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
355	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
356	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
357	  \]
358	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
359	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
360	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
361	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
362	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
363	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
364	  \[
365	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
366	    \qquad
367	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
368	  \]
369	  the associator
370	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
371	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
372	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
373	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
374	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
375	\end{lemma}
376	
377	\begin{proof}
378	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
379	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
380	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
381	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
382	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
383	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
384	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
385	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
386	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
387	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
388	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
389	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
390	\end{proof}
391	
392	\begin{remark}
393	[The full monoidal category on all of $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$ is off-path; the group law consumes only propositions]
394	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
395	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality, lem:whisker_of_W}
396	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
397	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
398	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
399	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
400	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
401	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. This is explicitly \emph{off-path}
402	  and \emph{not pursued}. One could in principle obtain it as the output of
403	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
404	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
405	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), supplying the instance
406	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}); but the group law never
407	  consumes a coherent monoidal category, so this construction is superseded and not
408	  carried out. The substrate provides only the \emph{existence} of the three
409	  coherence isomorphisms that the group law actually consumes.
410	
411	  Were that superseded route pursued, the instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\)
412	  would need \emph{no} closed structure: its
413	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
414	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
415	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W} (a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
416	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
417	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\)), the same technique Mathlib uses for
418	  fixed-base presheaves in
419	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\). It would use neither
420	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
421	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \texttt{flat\_whisker\_localizer} (which is
422	  false over a non-affine open and is likewise off the critical path). The \emph{full}
423	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance and the \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal
424	  category are not on the critical path: the group law is realized without them. The
425	  single left-whisker existence lemma and its underlying d.1/d.2 stalk apparatus, by
426	  contrast, \emph{are} on the critical path, since the associator's sheafification
427	  transport consumes exactly that one whisker obligation
428	  (\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk}, closed via d.2).
429	
430	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
431	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
432	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
433	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
434	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
435	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
436	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
437	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
438	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
439	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, supplied directly: the associator by gluing canonical
440	  local isomorphisms through the restriction-compatibility isomorphism
441	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), and the
442	  unitors and braiding by the cheap \(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern
443	  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}). The carrier --- iso-classes
444	  of the substrate tensor \(\otimes_X\) --- is the scheme-theoretic analogue of
445	  Mathlib's Picard group \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R := \mathrm{Skeleton}(
446	  \mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}), but it
447	  is built \emph{differently}: Mathlib reads its \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) off the
448	  \(\mathrm{Skeleton}\) of the coherent monoidal category
449	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalCategory}(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\,R)\) over a fixed ring,
450	  whereas here --- with no coherent monoidal category on
451	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) available for the varying structure sheaf --- the
452	  group is assembled by hand from the existence-of-isomorphism facts.
453	\end{remark}
454	
455	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
456	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
457	
458	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
459	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
460	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
461	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
462	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section records the
463	\emph{existence-of-isomorphism facts on line bundles} (associativity, unit,
464	commutativity and inverse) that the relative Picard group law consumes. These facts
465	are supplied \emph{directly}: associativity by the direct-gluing associator
466	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} (through the linchpin
467	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}), unit and commutativity by the cheap
468	\(\mathtt{mapIso}\) pattern (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}),
469	and the inverse existentially from \(\otimes\)-invertibility. They are \emph{not}
470	read off any \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure: the route that would derive them from
471	\texttt{jw\_ismonoidal} (route~(e)) is superseded and off-path. The section also
472	records the closure of \(\otimes\) on the locally-trivial
473	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype (\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}) and
474	the interaction with the pullback functor \(\pi_T^*\). The open-immersion
475	restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is on the
476	critical path precisely because it is consumed by the direct-gluing associator
477	\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}; it is \emph{not} tied to the superseded
478	whiskering-stability lemma \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}, which belongs
479	to the off-path route~(e).
480	
481	\begin{lemma}
482	
483	\leanok
484	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
485	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
486	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
487	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
488	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
489	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
490	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
491	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
492	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
493	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
494	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
495	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
496	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}
497	  rings on a site (so that both \(R\) and \(S\) factor through
498	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and the category of presheaves of modules over each
499	  is monoidal). Then the presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor
500	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) is lax monoidal.
501	  Consequently, composing with the already-monoidal Mathlib functor
502	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) via
503	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\), the full pushforward
504	  \[
505	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi
506	      \;\;:=\;\;
507	    \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \;\circ\; \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi
508	  \]
509	  is lax monoidal. This lemma is a self-contained
510	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level lax-monoidal supplement: it stands on its own
511	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
512	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of
513	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, whose proof identifies restriction along an
514	  open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
515	  consequently needs no lax-monoidal structure on the pushforward.
516	\end{lemma}
517	
518	\begin{proof}
519	
520	\leanok
521	  This is a \emph{sectionwise} lift of the existing fact that, for a ring map
522	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
523	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
524	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
525	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
526	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
527	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
528	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
529	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
530	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
531	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
532	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
533	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
534	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
535	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a
536	  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreLaxMonoidal}\) yields the lax-monoidal structure; no
537	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
538	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
539	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
540	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a definitional
541	  transport across the unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).
542	
543	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
544	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
545	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
546	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
547	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
548	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
549	\end{proof}

551	\begin{lemma}
552	
553	\leanok
554	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
555	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
556	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
557	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
558	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
559	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
560	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
561	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
562	  morphism
563	  \[
564	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
565	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
566	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
567	  \]
568	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
569	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
570	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
571	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
572	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
573	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products.
574	\end{lemma}
575	
576	\begin{proof}
577	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
578	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
579	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
580	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
581	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
582	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
583	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
584	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
585	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
586	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
587	  isomorphism}
588	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
589	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
590	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
591	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
592	  therefore extension of scalars along
593	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
594	
595	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
596	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
597	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
598	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
599	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
600	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for

646	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
647	  \texttt{lemma-exactness-pushforward-pullback}: pullback and pushforward of
648	  $\mathcal{O}$-modules along a morphism form an adjoint pair $(f^*, f_*)$, the

651	  \emph{Step 3 (the genuine residual: H1 alone).} Completing this comparison at the
652	  presheaf level requires a sectionwise handle on the abstract functor
653	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), which is defined as the left
654	  adjoint of pushforward (the presheaf-of-modules avatar of the
655	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) adjunction of the cited Stacks lemma) and admits no
656	  sectionwise formula in Mathlib at the pinned commit. The closure passes through
657	  two presheaf-level ingredients, of which exactly one now remains open.
658	
659	  The first ingredient --- a strong-monoidal upgrade of
660	  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the structure-sheaf ring \emph{isomorphism}
661	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), so that base change along the isomorphism commutes with
662	  \(\otimes\) --- is \emph{closed in this chapter} as
663	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
664	  (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}\)): for a ring isomorphism
665	  \(e : R \simeq S\) the canonical map \(a \otimes_R b \mapsto a \otimes_S b\) is an
666	  \(R\)-linear equivalence \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R
667	  \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M
668	  \otimes_S N)\).
669	
670	  The \emph{sole remaining residual} is therefore the presheaf-level identification
671	  \[
672	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
673	  \]
674	  via \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\). One exhibits a
675	  presheaf-level adjunction
676	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) and concludes
677	  that \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\), being a left adjoint of
678	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), is canonically isomorphic to the abstract left
679	  adjoint \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\). Here \(\beta\) is the structure map of the
680	  restriction functor, so that \((M|_f).\mathrm{val} =
681	  (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M.\mathrm{val}\) definitionally;
682	  composing the resulting comparison with
683	  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv} yields the asserted isomorphism.
684	
685	  This presheaf-level adjunction is the presheaf analogue of the sheaf-level
686	  \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\), and must be built from
687	  presheaf-level \(\mathtt{pushforwardNatTrans}\) and
688	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardCongr}\). These two building blocks are \emph{Mathlib-absent}
689	  at the pinned commit: only the \emph{sheaf}-level versions exist
690	  (\texttt{Mathlib/.../SheafOfModules/Sheaf/PushforwardContinuous.lean}), whereas
691	  \(\mathtt{pushforwardId}\) and \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) do already exist at the
692	  presheaf level. This residual is being constructed \emph{directly}, mirroring the
693	  existing sheaf-level template app-for-app at the presheaf level (a Mathlib-gradient
694	  build, not a deferral to an upstream PR).

696	  This lemma is the single substrate linchpin of the relative Picard group law for
697	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(M, N\): the associator
698	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} consumes the comparison
699	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_f \cong M|_f \otimes_U N|_f\) on its sole consumer
700	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}, where the restriction is commuted
701	  past the \emph{sheafified} tensor \(M \otimes_X N\) \emph{before} any local
702	  trivialisation of \(M, N\) is introduced --- so the comparison is required for the
703	  global, untrivialised modules, and no free-cover specialisation can stand in for it.
704	  Consequently the H1 residual \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong
705	  \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) is genuinely on the critical path, and closing it is
706	  the \emph{single open substrate obligation} of this chapter. The superseded
707	  route~(e) \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal} \to \mathtt{Sheaf.monoidalCategory}\)
708	  (\texttt{jw\_ismonoidal}) remains named only as a last-resort fallback should the
709	  presheaf-level adjunction itself bottom out; it is not the plan.
710	\end{proof}

714	\leanok
715	[Base change along a ring isomorphism commutes with $\otimes$]
716	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv}
717	  \lean{restrictScalarsRingIsoTensorEquiv}
718	  Let \(e : R \simeq S\) be an isomorphism of commutative rings, and let \(M, N\) be
719	  \(S\)-modules. Restriction of scalars along \(e\) commutes with the tensor
720	  product: the canonical map
721	  \[
722	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(M) \otimes_R \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e(N)
723	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
724	    \mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\bigl(M \otimes_S N\bigr),
725	    \qquad a \otimes_R b \;\longmapsto\; a \otimes_S b,
726	  \]
727	  is an \(R\)-linear equivalence. Equivalently, \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}_e\) is
728	  \emph{strong} monoidal, not merely lax, when \(e\) is a ring \emph{isomorphism}:
729	  base change along an isomorphism \(R \simeq S\) identifies the two ground rings, so
730	  the lax structure map of \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\) is invertible. The
731	  forward map is \(R\)-linear, but its inverse \(a \otimes_S b \mapsto a \otimes_R b\)
732	  is only \emph{additive} (it is not \(S\)-linear), so the inverse is constructed as
733	  a homomorphism of additive groups and then verified to be the two-sided inverse of
734	  the forward \(R\)-linear map. This closes the strong-monoidal ingredient of
735	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, leaving the presheaf-level pushforward
736	  adjunction as the sole open residual there.
737	\end{lemma}

742	[Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial]
743	  \label{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
744	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial}
745	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
746	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, and let
747	  \(L, L' \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be locally-trivial modules of
748	  rank one (i.e.\ line bundles). The tensor product \(L \otimes_X L'\) of
749	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} is again locally trivial of rank
750	  one. Consequently the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) restricts to a bifunctor
751	  on the full subcategory \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X \subset \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
752	  of locally-trivial rank-one modules.
753	\end{lemma}
754	
755	\begin{proof}
756	
757	  Pick a common affine open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) on which both
758	  \(L|_{U_i}\) and \(L'|_{U_i}\) are free of rank one. On each \(U_i\)
759	  there are trivialisations \(L|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and
760	  \(L'|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\). The tensor product of two free
761	  rank-one modules over a commutative ring is again free of rank one;
762	  hence \((L \otimes_X L')|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\). So
763	  \(L \otimes_X L'\) is locally trivial of rank one, i.e.\ an object of
764	  \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X\).
765	\end{proof}

840	\section{Route~(e): the \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal structure}
841	\label{sec:tensorobj_route_e}
842	
843	\emph{Full-monoidal-category construction --- off path, not to be formalized.} The
844	construction below realizes a \emph{full} symmetric monoidal category on all of
845	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) by instantiating Mathlib's
846	localization-of-monoidal-categories API. It was the original plan for supplying the
847	associator. The relative Picard group law, however, is a structure on
848	\emph{isomorphism classes} of invertible sheaves and consumes only the
849	\emph{existence} of the associator, unitor and braiding isomorphisms
850	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}, built by hand as the scheme-theoretic
851	analogue of Mathlib's \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\), not off a coherent monoidal
852	category's \(\mathtt{Skeleton}\)). The associator is obtained as the sheafification
853	transport of the presheaf associator (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}), whose
854	\emph{single} open left-whisker obligation is closed by the d.2 stalk--tensor
855	commutation (\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk,%
856	lem:stalk_tensor_commutation}); it uses \emph{no} \emph{full}
857	\((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance and \emph{no} \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\)
858	monoidal category. Accordingly the \emph{full monoidal-category} target of this
859	section --- the \emph{pair} of whiskering-stability fields (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W})
860	and the \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) instance (\texttt{jw\_ismonoidal}) --- is off
861	path and must not be re-attempted; it is kept for the historical record. The d.1/d.2
862	stalk apparatus it shares with the live single whisker obligation is \emph{not}
863	vestigial, however: it is on the critical path through
864	\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk}.
865	
866	The monoidal structure on the substrate is realized by \emph{instantiating}
867	Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
868	(\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}): apply
869	\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) to the already-monoidal
870	presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) and the
871	sheafification localizer \(J.W\). The single new input the API requires is the
872	instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), whose two fields are the
873	whiskering-stability lemmas below.
874	
875	\paragraph{What the group law consumes.} The group law is assembled directly on
876	isomorphism classes of \emph{locally trivial} (invertible) sheaves
877	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}), mirroring Mathlib's ring-level Picard
878	group \(\mathtt{Module.Invertible}\) / \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\)
879	(\texttt{Mathlib/RingTheory/PicardGroup.lean}). It needs only the \emph{existence} of
880	the associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, the unitors \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
881	and the braiding \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, together with \(\otimes\)-invertibility
882	supplying inverses; it uses \emph{no} \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance, \emph{no}
883	\(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal category, and \emph{no} \(\mathtt{Skeleton}\).
884	It does, however, consume the associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, which is
885	realized as the sheafification transport of the presheaf associator and whose
886	\emph{single} open left-whisker obligation \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk}
887	is closed by the stalk--tensor commutation (d.2, \cref{lem:stalk_tensor_commutation}).
888	Hence the stalk ingredients (d.1)/(d.2) are \emph{on} the critical path of the group
889	law --- through the associator's single whisker obligation --- even though the full
890	\(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal} \to \mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal category on all of
891	\(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) (the subject of the present section) is \emph{not} built:
892	that full monoidal generality, with its \emph{pair} of whiskering-stability fields
893	(\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}) and all derived coherence, is what the group law does not
894	consume. The single open obligation of the present section's full-monoidal target is
895	thus the local-injectivity half packaged for an arbitrary whisker object
896	(\cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}), itself a consequence of the same d.2
897	commutation.

2404	For composable scheme morphisms `h : Z ⟶ Y`, `f : Y ⟶ X` and any `Q : X.Modules`, the unit of the

2408	`unit_conjugateEquiv` combined with `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` (the mate of `pullbackComp.inv`

2412	directly.  Project-local. -/

2415	    (SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (h ≫ f))).unit.app Q ≫
2416	        (SheafOfModules.pushforward (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom (h ≫ f))).map
2417	          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp h f).inv.app Q) =
2418	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp
2419	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction h)).unit.app Q ≫
2420	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardComp h f).hom.app

2423	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).comp

1015	\leanok
1016	  \emph{PRIMARY route (\(F\) locally trivial --- the consumer's case; sheaf-level,
1017	  no stalks, no (d.2)).} Suppose \(F\) is locally trivial
1018	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,F\)), which is exactly the hypothesis its
1019	  sole consumer --- the associator \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} --- supplies.
1020	  \(J\)-local injectivity of a morphism is \emph{local on a covering family}: it
1021	  suffices to produce a cover of \(X\) on each member of which \(F \triangleleft g\)
1022	  restricts to a locally injective morphism. Take a trivialising open cover
1023	  \(\{V\}\) of \(X\) for \(F\), so that \(F|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V\) on each \(V\).
1024	  On such a \(V\):
1025	  \begin{itemize}
1026	    \item the substrate--restriction compatibility \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1027	      gives a canonical isomorphism
1028	      \[
1029	        (F \triangleleft g)\big|_V \;\cong\; (F|_V) \triangleleft (g|_V)
1030	      \]
1031	      --- restriction along the open immersion \(V \hookrightarrow X\) commutes with
1032	      \(\otimes\), and the whiskering \(F \triangleleft g = \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g\)
1033	      is \(\otimes\) with an identity, so it restricts to \(\mathrm{id}_{F|_V}
1034	      \otimes (g|_V) = (F|_V) \triangleleft (g|_V)\);
1035	    \item the trivialisation \(F|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V\) together with the left
1036	      unitor \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} (\(\mathcal{O}_V \otimes_V (-) \cong
1037	      (-)\)) identifies \((F|_V) \triangleleft (g|_V) \cong g|_V\).
1038	  \end{itemize}
1039	  Composing, \((F \triangleleft g)|_V\) is isomorphic to \(g|_V\). Now \(g \in J.W\),
1040	  and membership in \(J.W\) (local bijectivity) is stable under restriction along an
1041	  open immersion, so \(g|_V \in J.W\); in particular \(g|_V\) is \(J\)-locally
1042	  injective. An isomorphism preserves local injectivity, hence \((F \triangleleft
1043	  g)|_V\) is locally injective on \(V\). As the \(\{V\}\) cover \(X\) and local
1044	  injectivity is local on the cover, \(F \triangleleft g\) is \(J\)-locally
1045	  injective. \emph{No stalks and no (d.2) stalk--tensor commutation enter}; the
1046	  single comparison lemma \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (a presheaf-level
1047	  comparison, decomposed into its two open-immersion base-change halves) carries the
1048	  argument, and thereby moves \emph{onto} the critical path.
1049	
1050	  This is \emph{not} the section-level route that tensors a bare section map \(g(V)\)
1051	  --- only injective --- and stalls on a \(\mathrm{Tor}_1\)/flatness obligation. Here
1052	  the argument is sheaf-level and uses that \(g\) is locally \emph{bijective} (a
1053	  \(J.W\) morphism), reduced through the unitor to \(g|_V\) (again a \(J.W\)
1054	  morphism); a mere injection is never tensored, so no flatness is needed.
1055	
1056	  \emph{FALLBACK route (arbitrary \(F\); stalkwise, required only for the
1057	  full-generality route~(e) \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) path).} For an
1058	  \emph{arbitrary} (not necessarily locally trivial) \(F\), local injectivity is
1059	  proved stalkwise, by the argument Mathlib uses for fixed-base presheaves in
1060	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\)
1061	  (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}), ported to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\). A
1062	  morphism in \(J.W\) is, on the topological site of \(X\), a \emph{stalkwise
1063	  isomorphism}: at each point \(x\) the induced map on stalks \(g_x\) is an
1064	  isomorphism. The presheaf-of-modules tensor is sectionwise, so it commutes with
1065	  the stalk (a filtered colimit), giving
1066	  \[
1067	    (F \triangleleft g)_x
1068	      \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes_{R_x} g_x
1069	      \;\colon\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} M_x \;\longrightarrow\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} N_x .
1070	  \]
1071	  Tensoring an isomorphism \(g_x\) by the identity \(\mathrm{id}_{F_x}\) yields an
1072	  isomorphism --- no flatness is needed, since \(g_x\) is invertible, not merely
1073	  injective. Hence \(F \triangleleft g\) is a stalkwise isomorphism, in particular
1074	  locally bijective, in particular locally injective, for \emph{any} \(F\).
1075	
1076	  The stalkwise computation rests on three ingredients with distinct status at the
1077	  pinned commit:
1078	  \begin{enumerate}
1079	    \item[(d.1-done)] the \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear stalk-map packaging
1080	      (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearMap}\) and its companions, \cref{lem:stalk_linear_map}):
1081	      a morphism of presheaves of modules induces an \(R.\mathtt{stalk}\,x\)-linear
1082	      map on stalks, characterised on germs, that becomes a linear equivalence once
1083	      the underlying \(\mathtt{Ab}\)-stalk map is an isomorphism. This is built
1084	      project-side, axiom-clean, and furnishes the \(\mathrm{id} \otimes g_x\) step
1085	      above.
1086	    \item[(d.1-bridge)] (remaining) the site-\(J.W\) \(\Longleftrightarrow\)
1087	      stalkwise-iso characterisation on \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\): that a morphism lies
1088	      in \(J.W\) iff its stalk maps are isomorphisms at every point. The concrete
1089	      Mathlib assembly route uses the \(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\) structure
1090	      together with the topological stalk criteria --- local injectivity
1091	      \(\Longleftrightarrow\) injectivity on stalks and local surjectivity
1092	      \(\Longleftrightarrow\) surjectivity on stalks --- bridging the site-level
1093	      \(\mathtt{Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective}\) / \(\mathtt{IsLocallySurjective}\)
1094	      to the \(\mathtt{TopCat.Presheaf}\) stalk versions. This half is still
1095	      Mathlib-absent at the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) level.
1096	    \item[(d.2)] (remaining) the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x
1097	      \otimes_{R_x} B_x\) of the stalk (a filtered colimit) with the relative
1098	      module-presheaf tensor, under which \((F \triangleleft g)_x\) is identified
1099	      with \(\mathrm{lTensor}\,F_x\,(g_x) = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) (using
1100	      that \(\mathtt{tensorLeft}\) / \(\mathtt{tensorRight}\) preserve filtered
1101	      colimits over a module category). This is genuinely Mathlib-absent over a
1102	      \emph{varying} ring and is the largest remaining piece.
1103	  \end{enumerate}

1133	[Flatness-free whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer]
1134	  \label{lem:whisker_of_W}
1135	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_W}
1136	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
1137	  With notation as in \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}: for an
1138	  \emph{arbitrary} presheaf of \(R\)-modules \(F\) and a morphism \(g\) whose
1139	  underlying additive-presheaf map lies in \(J.W\), both whiskerings again lie in
1140	  \(J.W\):
1141	  \[
1142	    g \in J.W \;\Longrightarrow\; F \triangleleft g \in J.W,
1143	    \qquad
1144	    g \in J.W \;\Longrightarrow\; g \triangleright F \in J.W .
1145	  \]
1146	  These two statements are precisely the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) and
1147	  \(\mathtt{whiskerRight}\) data required by the class
1148	  \(\mathtt{MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}) for
1149	  the morphism property \(J.W\); together they furnish the instance
1150	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) of \texttt{jw\_ismonoidal}. No flatness and no
1151	  local triviality is used --- they are the flatness-free replacements for the
1152	  flat variants of \texttt{flat\_whisker\_localizer}.
1153	\end{lemma}

1155	\begin{proof}
1156	  \uses{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
1157	  \leanok
1158	  By the locally-bijective characterisation of \(J.W\), it suffices to show
1159	  \(F \triangleleft g\) is both locally injective and locally surjective.
1160	  \emph{Local surjectivity} is free and needs no hypothesis on \(F\): the presheaf
1161	  tensor is right exact, so \(F \otimes^{p} -\) carries the locally-zero cokernel
1162	  of \(g\) to the cokernel of \(F \triangleleft g\), which is therefore locally
1163	  zero (Mathlib \(\mathtt{isLocallySurjective\_whiskerLeft}\)). \emph{Local
1164	  injectivity} is \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}. The two together
1165	  give \(F \triangleleft g \in J.W\). The right-whiskered statement
1166	  \(g \triangleright F\) follows by conjugating \(F \triangleleft g\) with the
1167	  braiding of the symmetric presheaf monoidal structure, which is an isomorphism
1168	  and hence preserves membership in \(J.W\).
1169	\end{proof}
1170	
1171	\emph{Superseded route --- off path, not to be formalized.} The following lemma is
1172	the route~(e) target: a full \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on all of
1173	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) from \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\). It is not
1174	needed. The group law consumes only the existence of the associator, unitor and
1175	braiding isomorphisms (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}, assembled by hand
1176	rather than off a coherent monoidal category's \(\mathtt{Skeleton}\)), and the
1177	associator is now built directly by
1178	gluing (\cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}, via \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}). This
1179	block is retained for the historical record only and must \emph{not} be formalized.
1180	
1181	\begin{lemma}
1182	
1183	\leanok
1184	[Associator for $\otimes_X$ (unconditional)]
1185	  \label{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso}
1186	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso}
1187	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk, lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}
1188	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
1189	  \(M, N, P \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). There is an isomorphism
1190	  \[
1191	    (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P
1192	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1193	    M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)
1194	  \]
1195	  in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), natural in \(M, N, P\). The group law on
1196	  \(\otimes\)-iso-classes (\cref{lem:tensorobj_isoclass_commgroup}) consumes only
1197	  the \emph{existence} of this isomorphism, the proposition
1198	  \(\mathrm{Nonempty}\bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \cong M \otimes_X (N
1199	  \otimes_X P)\bigr)\); no pentagon, triangle, or naturality coherence of it is
1200	  ever required.
1201	
1202	  The Lean pin is unconditional: it holds for arbitrary
1203	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(M, N, P\), with no local-triviality or
1204	  invertibility hypothesis. (Earlier drafts carried vestigial
1205	  \(\mathtt{IsLocallyTrivial}\) hypotheses to match the carrier; these were
1206	  dropped once the construction below was seen to use none of them.)
1207	\end{lemma}
1208	
1209	\begin{proof}
1210	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk}
1211	  \textbf{Realization (sheafification transport, unconditional via d.2).} The
1212	  associator is the sheafification transport of the presheaf-of-modules associator
1213	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct.associator}\), which exists for
1214	  \emph{all} presheaves of modules over the varying ring. The transport requires the
1215	  single left-whisker \(F \triangleleft \mathtt{toSheafify}\) to lie in the
1216	  sheafification localizer \(J.W\) for the relevant \(F\); that single open
1217	  left-whiskering obligation \(\mathtt{isLocallyInjective\_whiskerLeft\_of\_W}\) is
1218	  closed \emph{unconditionally} --- for an arbitrary whiskering object, with no
1219	  flatness and no local triviality --- by the d.2 stalk--tensor commutation
1220	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk} (\cref{sec:tensorobj_stalk_tensor}):
1221	  a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a stalkwise isomorphism, and by the d.2 commutation
1222	  \(\bigl(F \triangleleft g\bigr)_x \cong \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes g_x\) is again an
1223	  isomorphism, so \(F \triangleleft g \in J.W\). Hence the associator is itself
1224	  unconditional and axiom-clean once d.2 (\cref{lem:stalk_tensor_commutation}) is
1225	  formalized; it does \emph{not} require the full \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance
1226	  or the \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal category --- only the one left-whisker
1227	  lemma --- and no sectionwise flatness enters. The local-gluing description below
1228	  records the equivalent concrete local realization through the
1229	  restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
1230	
1231	  Concretely, the same isomorphism is described by gluing canonical local isomorphisms
1232	  through the restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
1233	  Fix a
1234	  common open cover \(\{U\}\) of \(X\). On each member \(U\) form the canonical
1235	  composite
1236	  \[
1237	    \bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P\bigr)\big|_U
1238	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1239	    \bigl(M|_U \otimes_U N|_U\bigr) \otimes_U P|_U
1240	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1241	    M|_U \otimes_U \bigl(N|_U \otimes_U P|_U\bigr)
1242	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1243	    \bigl(M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\bigr)\big|_U ,
1244	  \]
1245	  in which:
1246	  \begin{itemize}
1247	    \item the first arrow is two applications of the restriction-compatibility
1248	      isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} along the open immersion
1249	      \(U \hookrightarrow X\): first \((M \otimes_X N)|_U \cong M|_U \otimes_U
1250	      N|_U\) tensored with \(P|_U\), then \(\bigl((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X
1251	      P\bigr)|_U \cong (M \otimes_X N)|_U \otimes_U P|_U\);
1252	    \item the middle arrow is the \emph{canonical presheaf-level associator}
1253	      \(\alpha\) of the (symmetric) monoidal category
1254	      \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_U\)
1255	      (\(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct.associator}\),
1256	      \cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}(i)), read on the restricted modules;
1257	    \item the third arrow is again two applications of
1258	      \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, in the opposite direction.
1259	  \end{itemize}
1260	  Each of the three arrows is a \emph{canonical} isomorphism: the
1261	  restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is induced
1262	  by the open immersion with no choices, hence natural in its module arguments, and
1263	  the presheaf associator \(\alpha\) is a fixed natural transformation. Therefore,
1264	  on a double overlap \(U \cap U'\), restricting the \(U\)-composite and the
1265	  \(U'\)-composite to \(U \cap U'\) yields the same morphism: each of the three
1266	  arrows commutes with the further restriction to \(U \cap U'\) by the naturality of
1267	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} and of the presheaf associator, so the two local
1268	  composites agree on the overlap.
1269	
1270	  Because the internal hom \(\mathcal{H}om(-,-)\) of sheaves of
1271	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is itself a sheaf on \(X\), a family of morphisms defined
1272	  on the members of an open cover and agreeing on the overlaps glues to a single
1273	  global morphism. Applying this both to the local composites above and to their
1274	  local inverses produces a global morphism
1275	  \((M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \to M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\) whose restriction
1276	  to each \(U\) is the local composite, together with a two-sided inverse glued from
1277	  the local inverses; hence the glued morphism is a global isomorphism --- the
1278	  associator. The single non-formal input of this re-route is the
1279	  restriction-compatibility comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}; the presheaf
1280	  associator (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}(i)) and the sheaf-of-modules
1281	  internal-hom gluing are already available.
1282	
1283	  Note that, unlike the pointwise-existence statement
1284	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial} --- a \emph{proposition} proved
1285	  cover-pointwise with \emph{no} overlap obligation --- the associator is
1286	  \emph{global data}, a single isomorphism of sheaves on all of \(X\). Assembling it
1287	  from local pieces is therefore genuinely stronger than that pattern: the
1288	  overlap-naturality compatibility of the local composites must be discharged before
1289	  gluing, and is discharged above. The locally-trivial hypotheses of the statement
1290	  are not consumed; the comparison \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is applied to the
1291	  global, untrivialised restrictions \((M \otimes_X N)|_U\), so no free-cover
1292	  specialisation can replace it. What this proof does \emph{not} require is the
1293	  \emph{full} \((J.W).\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) instance or the
1294	  \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\) monoidal category
1295	  (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}): only the single left-whisker obligation
1296	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk}, closed via the d.2 stalk--tensor
1297	  commutation \cref{lem:stalk_tensor_commutation}, is consumed by the sheafification
1298	  transport. The group law on \(\otimes\)-iso-classes
1299	  consumes only the \emph{existence} of the resulting isomorphism, never its pentagon,
1300	  triangle, or naturality coherence.
1301	\end{proof}

1307	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}

1320	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}

1322	  the left and right unitors \(\lambda, \rho\) at the presheaf level. Their
1323	  sheafifications \(\mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}(\lambda)\),

1332	\begin{lemma}

1334	\leanok

1355	\begin{lemma}
1356	
1357	\leanok
1358	[Inverse of an invertible module]
1359	  \label{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}
1360	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse}
1361	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1362	  % NOTE: ON the critical path under the PRIMARY locally-trivial group law (it

1374	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1375	  % "\begin{lemma}
1376	  % \label{lemma-constructions-invertible}

1393	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR, Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
1394	  invertible} (item~2, the dual of an invertible is invertible and the contraction

1398	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(L \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,X\) be a line
1399	  bundle. The dual
1400	  \[
1401	    L^{-1} \;\;:=\;\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)

1403	  is again a line bundle on \(X\), and there is a canonical natural
1404	  isomorphism

1409	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
1410	\end{lemma}
1411	
1412	\begin{proof}
1413	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:internal_hom_isSheaf, lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial, lem:dual_restrict_iso}
1414	  The dual object and its evaluation are now supplied by the internal-hom
1415	  infrastructure of \cref{sec:tensorobj_dual_infra}. Take
1416	  \(L^{-1} := \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)\) to be the
1417	  sheaf-level dual \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} --- a genuine object of
1418	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), assembled from the presheaf internal hom
1419	  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom} by sheafification --- and let
1420	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \to \mathcal{O}_X\) be the evaluation
1421	  (contraction) morphism \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}. With both in hand the
1422	  argument is three steps.
1423	
1424	  \emph{Step 1 (\(L^{-1}\) is a line bundle).} Local triviality of \(L\)
1425	  (\(\mathtt{LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial}\,L\)) furnishes an open cover \(\{U\}\) of
1426	  \(X\) with trivialisations \(L|_U \cong \mathcal{O}_U\). The internal hom commutes
1427	  with restriction along the open immersion \(U \hookrightarrow X\), so on each \(U\)
1428	  \[
1429	    L^{-1}\big|_U \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(L|_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
1430	                 \;\cong\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{O}_U)
1431	                 \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U ,
1432	  \]
1433	  the last identification being the evaluation-at-\(1\) isomorphism for the dual of a
1434	  free rank-one module. Hence \(L^{-1}\) is locally trivial of rank one, i.e.\ an
1435	  object of \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X\); this is exactly
1436	  \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}.
1437	
1438	  \emph{Step 2 (the contraction is a local isomorphism).} The evaluation/contraction
1439	  morphism
1440	  \[
1441	    \varepsilon_L \;\colon\; L \otimes_X L^{-1}
1442	      \;=\; L \otimes_X \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
1443	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_X,
1444	    \qquad s \otimes \phi \longmapsto \phi(s),
1445	  \]
1446	  is a morphism in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Restricting to a trivialising
1447	  open \(U\), the restriction-compatibility isomorphism \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
1448	  commutes \(\otimes_X\) past \((-)|_U\):
1449	  \[
1450	    (L \otimes_X L^{-1})\big|_U \;\cong\; L|_U \otimes_U L^{-1}|_U
1451	      \;\cong\; \mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U ,
1452	  \]
1453	  using the trivialisations of Step~1. Under these identifications \(\varepsilon_L|_U\)
1454	  becomes the contraction \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U \to \mathcal{O}_U\) of
1455	  the free rank-one module with its dual, which is precisely the left unitor
1456	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} (\(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U (-) \cong (-)\)) and hence an
1457	  isomorphism. Thus \(\varepsilon_L|_U\) is an isomorphism on each member of the cover.
1458	
1459	  \emph{Step 3 (glue to a global isomorphism).} The local trivialisations are induced
1460	  by the open immersions with no choices, so the local contraction isomorphisms
1461	  \(\varepsilon_L|_U\) agree on the overlaps \(U \cap U'\) by the naturality of
1462	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} and of the unitor under further restriction. As
1463	  ``being an isomorphism'' is local on \(X\) --- a morphism of sheaves of
1464	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose restriction to every member of an open cover is an
1465	  isomorphism is itself an isomorphism --- the globally-defined \(\varepsilon_L\) is an
1466	  isomorphism \(L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\). Equivalently, the
1467	  local inverses glue (they agree on overlaps for the same reason) to a two-sided
1468	  inverse of \(\varepsilon_L\).
1469	
1470	  Consequently every line bundle \(L\) has the two-sided tensor inverse \(L^{-1}\)
1471	  under \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid of \(\otimes\)-iso-classes of line bundles is
1472	  an abelian group --- the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR). The
1473	  local-triviality of \(L^{-1}\) (Step~1) is \cref{lem:dual_isLocallyTrivial}, the
1474	  dual object and its evaluation are \cref{lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} and
1475	  \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}, and the local-iso\(\Rightarrow\)global-iso passage
1476	  (Step~3) is the already-closed \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}.
1477	
1478	  \emph{Downstream Lean dependency.} The formal body of

1485	  closes as a routine downstream consequence the moment \cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}
1486	  is discharged; no further infrastructure is required.
1487	\end{proof}
